feed.title: SAP Community - Cloud Integration
feed.subtitle: Cloud Integration Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Read Multiple Attachments in CPI
entry.content:
I have followed this guide line to send email for each attachment. https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/read-multiple-attachments?locale=en-US

The system is sending email for each attachment either with all attachment or Zero attachment.

The purpose is to send each attachment is one email.

Here is first groovy script properties 

AttachmentCount  = 2

AttachmentList = {39AHD.pdf=javax.activation.DataHandler@152e7c48, 0395_20260518135718.102_X.pdf=javax.activation.DataHandler@3359a48c} 

Process loop condition ${property.AttachmentCount} > '0'

I have attached iflow

entry.title: issue while updating pricing conditions for NBIC Purchase Orders using API_PURCHASEORDER_2
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue while updating pricing conditions for NBIC Purchase Orders using API_PURCHASEORDER_2 in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

Scenario:

* PO Type: NBIC
* API: API_PURCHASEORDER_2
* Entity: PurOrderItemPricingElement
* Condition Type: PMP0

I am able to successfully PATCH the pricing condition using Postman and receive HTTP 200 OK response, but the value is not actually updated in the database. After re-reading the pricing element, the old value is restored automatically.

PATCH URL:

/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_purchaseorder_2/srvd_a2x/sap/purchaseorder/0001/PurOrderItemPricingElement(
PurchaseOrder='4500000450',
PurchaseOrderItem='00010',
PricingDocument='1000000667',
PricingDocumentItem='000010',
PricingProcedureStep='060',
PricingProcedureCounter='001'
)

Payload:

{
"ConditionRateAmount": 159.000000000,
"ConditionCurrency": "AED",
"ConditionIsManuallyChanged": true
}

Response:

* HTTP 200 OK
* No API error

However, after GET:

* ConditionRateAmount still shows old value
* Pricing seems to be redetermined automatically

Observed fields from GET response:

{
"ConditionControl": "C",
"ConditionOrigin": "C",
"ConditionIsDeletable": false,
"ConditionType": "PMP0"
}

Additional findings:

1. POST on PurOrderItemPricingElement is not supported
Error:
"Creating operations are disabled for entity"

2. PATCH on PurchaseOrderItem with NetPriceAmount also returns:
"Operation is not enabled"

3. Same API update works correctly for NB Purchase Orders, but not for NBIC.

My questions:

1. Is pricing condition update intentionally restricted for NBIC Purchase Orders in Public Cloud?
2. Are PMP0/intercompany conditions protected from API persistence?
3. Is there any supported approach to update pricing for NBIC POs through API?
4. Should pricing only be updated indirectly through condition records/info records?

Any guidance or SAP documentation reference would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: How to reuse or modularize JavaScript in SAP CPI?
entry.content:
Introduction

As described in the article Modularising CPI Groovy scripts using POGO , there is a concept of splitting code into smaller modules by using multiple scripts within an ifFow. However, the explanation applies only to Groovy.

I am looking for a similar approach using JavaScript, which is also available in SAP CPI.

POC - additional function outside of 'processData'

As a first step, I prepared POC using a single script. It contains the main function (processData) and an additional helper function defined outside of it. Script below : 

This approach works correctly. After testing via Postman, i received the expected results. 

POC - additional code as a seperate script (failure result)

In the next step, I tried to move the helper logic into separate script to improve readability and maintainability.. So i copied the code into a new script called "JS_ExtScript". Then in my main script "JS_withExtScript", I attempted to use the "importClass" syntax with the path src.main.resources.script.<script_name>.<class_name> .

However, the tests ended with an error . 

 

I did some research and found the following resources: 

- Try use JavaScript scripting instead of Groovy scripting in your CPI project , where someone from SAP recommended to check functions like defineClass, load, loadClass, but none of these worked in my case. 

- SAP Integration Suites javascript load library, similar question regarding my topic was raised, but no clear solution was provided. The author ended up keepin all logic in a single script, similar to my initial POC which works fine but this is not what i am looking for.

 

Has anyone successfully implemented modular JavaScript in SAP CPI (e.g. using multiple scripts or reusable libraries)?
Any suggestions or workarounds would be greatly appreciated.

Looking for you help. Thanks in advance !

entry.title: How to import a Groovy class reference in Resources->References in CPI?
entry.content:
As shown in Modularising CPI Groovy scripts using POGO | SAP Blogs classes that are in other scripts are available by importing them with:

import src.main.resources.script.ClassName

They just sitting in:

 

What happens when I put such a class in a separate package? What is the import path then?

 

I tried to move the class directly to a file in Common Script Collection and the import doesn't work. When the class is in the Local file the import works.

entry.title: Cloud Integration metering for outbound payloads >250KB does not increase Total Messages
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are performing controlled transaction metering tests in SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) and we found a behavior that seems inconsistent with the official documentation regarding payloads larger than 250KB.

Scenario

Integration Flow:

HTTPS Sender → XML to JSON Transformation → HTTP Receiver Adapter → External Node.js API

Additionally, the iFlow performs another outbound HTTP call to an external monitoring API.

The flow does NOT contain:

  • Splitter
  • Multicast
  • JMS
  • DataStore
  • Retries

Test Execution

We executed the test using Postman Runner with:

  • 345 iterations

  • Inbound XML payload size ≈ 690 KB

  • Outbound JSON payload size after transformation ≈ 463 KB

We also verified the following exchange properties:

SAP_InboundMessageSizeInKB = 690
content-length ≈ 689558

Additionally, we logged the outbound payload before the HTTP receiver adapter:

OutboundSizeKB ≈ 463

Expected Behavior

According to SAP documentation:

“The combined (aggregated) size of all outbound transactions via one or more receiver(s) adapter is considered for transaction metering.”

“If it is more than 250KB, it will be counted in blocks of 250KB.”

Based on this rule, we expected:

463KB / 250KB ≈ 2 transactions per execution

Therefore:

345 executions ≈ 690 Total Messages

Observed Behavior

The Message Usage Dashboard shows:

  • MPL Count = 345

  • Total Messages = 345

Meaning:

  • each execution is counted as only 1 message

  • payload size >250KB does not appear to increase the Total Messages counter

Additional Observation

When testing integration flows with Splitter patterns, the Message Usage Dashboard DOES increase Total Messages correctly according to the number of split outbound transactions.

This suggests that:

  • Splitter-based outbound transactions are correctly metered

  • but outbound HTTP payload size >250KB in a simple HTTP receiver scenario may not be metered as expected

Question

Can someone clarify:

  1. Is transaction metering in Cloud Integration really based on outbound payload size blocks of 250KB for HTTP receiver adapters?

  2. Does the Message Usage Dashboard aggregate or simplify payload-based metering?

  3. Is this behavior different in BTP Trial vs productive tenants?

  4. Is the 250KB rule applied only under specific integration patterns?

Any clarification from SAP product experts or architects would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks.

Evidence:

Message Usage Dashboard

 

SAP Inbound & Outbound Size KB

 

iFlow Diagram

 

Splitter Comparison

entry.title: Error calling the OData V4 adapter for a RAP Odata service
entry.content:
When invoking the OData V4 adapter using the PATCH operation from the Integration Suite, I receive the following error: "EDMX Fetch failed with HTTP Response Code: 403." The OData service is based on a custom-developed managed RAP object.
The READ operation works, and directly invoking the OData service with PATCH via Postman also works. Therefore, I suspect this is an issue specific to the Integration Suite. What could be the cause?

entry.title: CPI IBMMQ vs AMQP
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are migrating from PO to CPI, and we have few IBM queues which does not require authorization. We tried using IBMMQ to connect to cloud connect via port 1414 but it's not hitting cloud connector via IBMMQ. It works fine with AMQP.

 

Is there any extra subscription we need to use IBMMQ adapter in CPI

entry.title: CAnnot configure Microsoft SharePoint Adapter for SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hello,

I cannot configure Microsoft SharePoint Adapter for SAP Integration Suite. 

I have copied it from Discover into Design, but cannot open it in anyways to configure it. We have standard plan so it should be available for use.

In actions for artifact there are only view metadata and deploy options available.

What

 

am I missing here? Authorizations?

Br,

Pekka

entry.title: .NET support for cloud connector, vertex,sftp,celonius
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We would like to know whether .NET is being used by SAP Cloud Connector, Vertex, SFTP, Celonis, or if it is just an independent component.

We need your support in confirming this because the current .NET versions we are using (6.0.22 / 23 / 24 / 25 / 29) will be reaching end of support soon.

Please let us know if any of these applications are dependent on the existing .NET version and whether an upgrade is required.

entry.title: CPI undeployed iflows(Neo Environment) to be cleaned in all the upper Environments
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have somany undeployed iflows in all the upper environments including Production. We are looking them to clean-up via CTS(Change and Transport System)  process..

 

Can you please let us know the process to be followed?.

 

Thanks & Regards,

Vasanth

entry.title: Integration Suite sharing within company group - how to organize content
entry.content:
Hi all,

I'm working for a company which is part of a group. Until now, each company of the group uses several CPIs on NEO. We will need to migrate to Integration Suite now. For costing reasons, there is one global account owned by the group and shared with all companies. The Integration Suite landscape will consist of two subaccounts (test and prod). These subaccounts will be shared with all companies of the group due to costing reasons.

As same IS subaccounts will be used by various companies, administration and development as well as monitoring will be handled by different people at different locations and working for different companies. If we do this in an uncontrolled manner, it will become a mess. 

We need to find a way on how to organize all the content, artifacts, partner details etc. in a meaningful manner. For this, I want to define some governance of how to organize artifacts, naming conventions, authorization rules etc.

As I'm quite new to IS, I'm struggling with defining such a governance. I found lots of learning content but most of them are somewhat of fundamental. Create one project, create first flow and that's it.
What I've learned so far is that there is no hierarchy within integration projects, like we do have in ABAP packages. As projects are the most fundamental part of organizing content, I wonder if we should organize it by company, integration scenario (CPI for C4C, C4s, S/4, non-SAP), business area (Sales, Production) or in a different way?

I didn't found any best practices and for this, I want to ask you to point me into the right direction. How do you handle complex integration landscapes? Do you have some link to a good resource to best practices?

entry.title: Unable to find SAP Cloud Platform Integration Library add-ons on sap store
entry.content: I cannot find my type library licenses on sap store using Joule nor search.   Neither is the page https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/managing-licence-for-type-systems-neo helping to find/ to purchase the ansi asc x12 library. I'm still on NEO.

entry.title: How to integrate and manage user data in SAP BTP Cockpit via SAP CPI ?
entry.content:
I am trying to design an integration flow to access and modify user data with specific roles in the SAP BTP Cockpit. However I noticed that there are no direct APIs available for managing cockpit user information.

1. What is the recommended approach to achieve this?

2. Are there alternative services, APIs available for this ?

3. Also I want all the information of the users like their last login times ?

Any guidance or references would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Automating Shipment Tracking Updates and Customer Notifications using SAP Integration Suite (CPI)
entry.content:
Introduction
In modern supply chain operations, customers expect timely updates regarding their shipments. This blog describes how SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) can be used to automate shipment tracking updates received from a third-party logistics provider and simultaneously notify customers via email. this will likely take 9–11 minutes to read end-to-end

Business Requirement

A third-party delivery partner, ECOM, receives shipment information from SAP. Once the shipment is processed, ECOM sends back shipment details including:

  • Tracking Number
  • Order Number
  • Shipment Status
  • Customer Name
  • Customer Email Address
  • Email Notification Flag

The requirement was to:

  1. Update the corresponding order information in SAP using an IDoc.
  2. Send an email notification to the customer containing the shipment tracking details.
  3. Ensure that customer notifications are sent only when the Email Notification Flag is enabled.

Solution Overview

The integration was developed using SAP Integration Suite – Cloud Integration (CPI).
Overview of the flow.

ECOM
↓ HTTPS (JSON)
SAP CPI
├─ IDoc → SAP
└─ Email → Customer

Source System :  ECOM sends shipment updates in JSON format through an HTTPS endpoint exposed by the CI iFlow.

Target Systems

  • SAP S4HANA via IDoc
  • Customer Email Inbox via Mail Adapter

Integration Flow Design:  The iFlow follows the below processing steps:

Step 1: HTTPS Sender Adapter
The shipment update is received from ECOM through an HTTPS endpoint in JSON format. you can always add an extra security by enabling csrf token protected here.

Step 2: JSON to XML Converter
The incoming JSON payload is converted into XML format to simplify downstream processing and mapping activities. I skipped adding XML root here because I am passing the root name in my source request. 

Step 3: Content Modifier
A Content Modifier is used to:

  • Set required header values using xpath expressions
  • Populate exchange properties for logging payload for example on or off.
  • Prepare values needed for routing and IDoc processing

Step 4: Groovy Script – Logging
A custom Groovy script is used to log the incoming payload and relevant message headers for monitoring and troubleshooting purposes.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message

def Message processData(Message message) {
def body = message.getBody(String)

def properties = message.getProperties()
def logPayload = properties.get("LogPayload")

def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
if (messageLog != null && logPayload == "true")
{
messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Payload Log", body, "text/plain")
}

return message
}

Step 5: Router
The router evaluates business conditions, particularly the notification flag.
Typical routing conditions:

  • Update SAP system
  • Send customer email if EmailNotificationFlag = true
  • also a default route in case the input is not meant to send to SAP or mail.

Route 1 – SAP Update Processing

Step 6: Message Mapping
The XML payload is mapped to the required SAP IDoc SHPMNT structure.
Mapping includes fields such as:

  • Order Number
  • Tracking Number
  • Shipment Status
  • SAP Delivery  Number

Step 7: Groovy Script – Payload Logging
An additional Groovy script logs the transformed IDoc payload before transmission to SAP.

Step 8: Request Reply
The Request Reply step ensures synchronous communication with SAP and captures the response.

Step 9: IDoc Receiver Adapter
The generated IDoc is sent to SAP S4HANA to update shipment tracking information.

Route 2 – Customer Notification Processing

Step 10: Send Step
The notification branch is triggered when the Email Notification Flag is enabled.

Step 11: Mail Adapter
The Mail Adapter sends an email directly to the customer containing:

  • Customer Name
  • Order Number
  • Tracking Number
  • Shipment Status

Mail Body:

<p>Dear Customer ${header.CustomerName},</p>
<p><br />You Order ${header.OrderNumber} has been shipped. Your shipment arrive soon.</p>
<p>Tracking Number is: ${header.OrderTrackingNumber}. You can use the tracking number to monitor the delivery status of your shipment.</p>
<p>For further support please reach out to our customer support.</p>
<p>Thank you for choosing our services.</p>
<p>Regards,</p>
<p>Customer Service Team.</p>
Sample Json Payload:
{
"PlannedStartTime": "10-06-2026T00:00:00",
"PlannedEndTime": "14-06-2026T00:00:00",
"TourNumber": "415487",
"OrderTrackingNumber": "TN12345678900",
"TransportOrderNumber": "2002121364",
"OrderStatus": "Shipped",
"SAPflag": true,
"Information": "Shipment created",
"ExternalNumber": "415487",
"Customer": {
"CustomerEmail": "customer@example.com",
"CustomerName": "John Doe",
"EmailNotificationFlag": true
}
}

Example email output content:

Dear Customer,
You Order 415487 has been shipped. Your shipment arrive soon.

Tracking Number is: TN12345678900, You can use the tracking number to monitor the delivery status of your shipment.

Thank you for choosing our services.
Regards,
Customer Service Team

Benefits of the Solution

  • Real-time shipment tracking updates
  • Automated SAP order synchronization
  • Improved customer communication
  • Reduced manual intervention
  • Better shipment visibility across systems

Conclusion
Using SAP Integration Suite, it is possible to efficiently orchestrate shipment updates from external logistics providers while simultaneously updating SAP business documents and keeping customers informed through automated email notifications. This approach improves operational efficiency and enhances the overall customer experience by providing timely and accurate shipment information.

Lessons Learned and Common Errors During Testing

During the development and testing of this integration scenario, several issues were encountered. The following troubleshooting notes may help others implementing similar solutions.

1. HTTP Status 403 – Forbidden

Error

HTTP Status 403 – Forbidden

Root Cause: The user invoking the HTTPS endpoint did not have the required authorization to send messages to the deployed integration flow.
Resolution: Ensure that the user calling the CPI endpoint has the required role assignment.
Required role: Once the role was assigned and the user re-authenticated, the HTTPS call was successfully processed.

MessagingSend

2. UnknownHostException while Calling SAP IDoc Service

Error

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException:
An internal server error occured:
UnknownHostException invoking
http://s4d:8010/sap/bc/srt/idoc?sap-client=120: s4d

Root Cause: SAP Integration Suite was unable to resolve or reach the backend SAP system.
Resolution: Verify the following configuration steps:

  1. The SAP backend system is correctly configured in SAP Cloud Connector.
  2. The virtual host and virtual port are maintained correctly.
  3. The exact IDoc service path is exposed and allowed in Cloud Connector.
  4. Connectivity tests between SAP Integration Suite and Cloud Connector are successful.
  5. The IDoc service is activated and registered in the SAP backend system.

After correcting the Cloud Connector configuration and exposing the required path, the IDoc communication worked successfully

3. SMTP Connectivity Error while Sending Email

Error

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException:
An internal server error occured:
Could not connect to SMTP host: smtp.gmail.com,
port: 465, response: -1

Root Cause: The mail adapter could not establish a secure connection with Gmail's SMTP server.
Resolution: Verify the following:

  1. Correct SMTP host and port configuration.
  2. Appropriate authentication method configured in the Mail Adapter.
  3. Gmail security settings allow SMTP access.
  4. App Passwords or OAuth authentication are configured according to Google's latest security requirements.
  5. TLS/SSL settings match the selected SMTP port.

Once the correct Gmail security configuration was implemented, email notifications were delivered successfully.

4. Malformed JSON Payload

Error

com.sap.it.rt.adapter.http.api.exception.HttpResponseException:
An internal server error occured:
Use JsonReader.setLenient(true) to accept malformed JSON
at line 11 column 20 path $.TourData.Tour[0].SAPflag

Root Cause: The incoming JSON payload contained invalid syntax in the SAPflag field. In this case, the issue was caused by incorrect input data during testing.
Resolution: Review the JSON payload and validate it using a JSON validator before sending it to SAP Integration Suite.

One thing worth highlighting is how clearly SAP Integration Suite identifies payload issues. The error message not only reported the parsing failure but also pinpointed the exact location of the problematic field:

$.TourData.Tour[0].SAPflag

This significantly reduced troubleshooting time and made root cause identification straightforward.

Final Thoughts

While the implementation itself was relatively straightforward, most challenges were related to connectivity, authorization, and payload validation. SAP Integration Suite provides detailed monitoring and Message Processing Logs (MPL), which greatly simplify issue analysis and resolution.

Hopefully, these lessons learned will help other developers accelerate their implementations and reduce troubleshooting efforts when integrating external logistics providers with SAP systems.

entry.title: Question regarding SAP BTP: Neo to Multi Environment migration
entry.content:
Hello SAP community,

maybe you could clarify something for me. We have two CPIs in the SAP BTP Neo environment. We want to migrate these into two separate Integration Suite Subaccounts, which are using the Multi environment. When I'm looking at the SAP Help Guides and SAP blogs regarding this topic, it is always said that you have to create a new Integration Suite subaccount. I want to specifically know, if that is necessary or if you can use an already used Integration Suite? If so, are there any precautions that need to be taken to not override any data (e.g. iFlows or Security Material) in the existing Integration Suite?

Best regards

entry.title: How to convert UTF-8 string to ISO-8859-1 using groovy?
entry.content:
Scenario is XML to EDIFACT where I want to convert the UTF-8 string from CPI to ISO-8859-1 

I have German characters like Ü and ß in the file. 

Script: 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
def Message processData(Message message) {
    String body = message.getBody(String)
    byte[] isoBytes = body.getBytes("ISO-8859-1")
    String encodedBody = new String(isoBytes, "ISO-8859-1")
    message.setBody(encodedBody)
    return message
}
 
I get the same output as the input. How to verify if the conversion is happening correctly?
 
I haven't tried below options as i'm not sure whether these are applicable for file adapter
    1. message.setHeader("Content-Type","text/plain;charset=ISO-8859-1") 
     2. message.setProperty("CamelCharsetName", "ISO-8859-1")

entry.title: Content Agent file upload Public API Returning 404 Not Found.
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are encountering an issue while calling the Content Agent File Upload API.

API Endpoint (taken from the Content Agent documentation):
https://content-agent-engine.cfapps.ap21.hana.ondemand.com/files/upload

Response:

{ "type": "about:blank", "title": "Not Found", "status": 404, "detail": "No static resource files/upload.", "instance": "/files/upload" }

We are using credentials from the Content Agent Service Application Plan to generate the access token and invoke the API.

The endpoint above was taken from the Content Agent API documentation. However, we are receiving a 404 Not Found response when attempting to access it.

Could you please investigate this issue and confirm whether the endpoint is correct and currently supported? If there is an updated endpoint, additional configuration required, or any prerequisite steps that we may have missed, please let us know.

entry.title: EMAIL oAUth Authentication issue
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are working on connecting from SAP Cloud Integration to a mail account in Outlook 365 via OAuth2 with Authorization Code grant type, using the protocol SMTP for sending e-mails .

Followed this blog Cloud Integration - Connect to Microsoft 365 Mail ... - SAP Community and completed the configuration also developed Generic Iflow for sending emails .But while we are testing ending up with error as Authorization unsuccessful 

 

Please find the detailed error logs in CPI

“javax.mail.AuthenticationFailedException: 535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful”.   Kindly advise on how to resolve this issue and what this error actually means.

entry.title: Custom Field addition to standard integration package - Package Integration SF EC to WFS
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement to add a custom field FO from Employee Central "Site" into mapping file of SAP CPI standard integration package - SuccessFactors Employee Central with Third Party Time Vendor-WorkForce Software(WFS) , Artifact - Packaged Integration SF EC to WFS, so that this information can be sent to Workforce System for business purposes.

We wanted to check if this is a functionality which can be performed in SAP CPI since the package integration says "configure-only" and not editable? Are there any KBAs around this?

Regards

Pooja Ganguly

entry.title: Has anyone done encoding in CPI with scripts(UTF-8 to any) or can it be handled by only adapters?
entry.content:
I observed that UTF-8 is the default standard for CPI and when try to convert to iso-8859-1 using script, i get a lot of issues including byte mismatch or character getting corrupted which raises me a question can the encoding be handled only at adapter level, please clarify?

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-convert-utf-8-string-to-iso-8859-1-using-groovy/qaq-p/14419909

If anyone have done any conversion internally in the flow level (like UTF-8 to any format like ANSI or iso 8859-1) which is working, please explain 

If not, please clarify which adapters supports encoding (i have observed that file adapters doesn't have that option, i hope http, soap and as2 should have the support, please add if i missed any)
